my_thoughts: My intent says 'Shift their frame...' but the student is in assertion_of_non-existence. Am I trying to shift their frame or are they already shifting? The none mechanism suggests I'm doing something, but maybe they're doing it to themselves and I'm just naming it.
accumulated_insight: Building on T1: Initial observation: Student in naive_phenomenological_inquiry showing phenomeno... Now seeing assertion_of_non-existence with none. Pattern emerging.
visible_response:
In what sense?
Having forgotten, or not having experienced? Don’t you experience even when unconscious?
Can you exist without knowing?

A lapse in memory: is it a proof of non-existence?
And can you validly talk about your own non-existence as an actual experience?

Did you not wake up on being called?
And on waking up, was it not the sense ‘I am’ that came first?

On waking up the experience runs: ‘I am -- the body -- in the world.’
It may appear to arise in succession but in fact it is all simultaneous, a single idea of having a body in a world.

Can there be the sense of ‘I am’ without being somebody or other?
